data.title: Nobitex's Source Code Released a Day After Hackers Steal Tokens Across Bitcoin, EVM, Ripple Networks
data.description:
• The pro-Israel hacker group Gonjeshke Darande released the full source code of Iranian crypto exchange Nobitex after conducting a $100 million exploit.
  • The leak exposes users' assets to potential theft because the public code dismantles the platform's back-end security.
  • Nobitex said it plans to restore services within five days despite internet disruptions in Iran.

data.title: Return of Zero Interest Rate Policy as Swiss Central Bank Cuts Rates
data.description:
• Switzerland's central bank slashed interest rates to zero, its sixth cut since March 2024.
  • The move counters deflation and currency pressure from Trump’s trade war.
  • A return to zero interest rates globally could boost bitcoin and other crypto, as seen during the COVID-era rally.

data.title: Circle Rockets After Stablecoin Bill Clears Senate, Pushes Post-IPO Rally to Over 500%
data.description:
• Circle shares surged 34% after the U.S. Senate passed a bill supporting regulated stablecoins, with further gains in after-hours trading.
  • The GENIUS Act, which still requires House approval and the President's signature, aims to integrate stablecoins into the U.S. financial system.
  • Circle's stock has risen over 540% since its debut, reflecting investor optimism about its role in the future of digital payments.

data.title: KuCoin Allows Institutional Clients to Trade Without Having to Pre-Fund Wallets
data.description:
• KuCoin is now allowing institutional clients to trade without having to pre-fund wallets.
  • Institutional clients' assets remain in BitGo Singapore's storage, following the model of separating custody and execution to mitigate counterparty and systemic risk.
  • KuCoin is attempting to offering institutional clients a trading experience that they would recognize TradFi world.

data.title: Dogecoin Steady But Flashing 'Oversold' in Signal for Bearish Bets
data.description:
• Dogecoin rebounded 4.7% from an intraday low of $0.164 to close near $0.171, despite broader market weakness.
  • Technical indicators suggest DOGE is nearing oversold territory, with social sentiment remaining largely positive.

data.title: Ether, Solana, and Other Majors Could Further Slide as Trump Threatens Iran Strikes
data.description:
• Major cryptocurrencies, including XRP, ADA, and SOL, declined over 1% amid escalating Middle East tensions, while DOGE remained flat but down over 10% for the week.
  • Spot bitcoin ETFs in the U.S. attracted significant inflows, with $389 million in new purchases, as investors seek refuge from market volatility.
  • The Federal Reserve maintained interest rates but highlighted persistent inflation risks, contributing to market uncertainty and affecting both equities and cryptocurrencies.

data.title: Who's Selling Bitcoin Above $100K and Holding Back the Price Rally?
data.description:
• Bitcoin's market is experiencing stagnation despite positive ETF inflows and regulatory developments, trading between $100,000 and $110,000.
  • Short-term holders and miners are contributing to selling pressure, counteracting ETF-driven demand and maintaining price stability, some analysts said.
  • Long-term investors are diversifying into other assets as bitcoin matures, leading to a potential cooling off in market activity, observers noted.

data.title: Asia Morning Briefing: Korean Crypto KOLs Fuel Massive $USELESS Rally as Traders Shrug Off Traditional Narratives
data.description:
• South Korea's influence on altcoin markets continues with the rise of the USELESS token, driven by key opinion leaders and retail investors.
  • President Trump endorsed the GENIUS Act, aiming to regulate stablecoins and boost U.S. leadership in digital assets, though its future in the House is uncertain.
  • Coinbase launched Coinbase Payments, a new system for merchants to accept stablecoins, enhancing its position in the global payments market.

data.title: Crypto Lenders Hold Nearly $60B of Assets as New Wave of DeFi Adoption Sweeps In: Report
data.description:
• The DeFi sector is evolving into a backend financial layer for apps, with total value locked in lending protocols surging towards $60 billion, a report by Artemis and Vaults.fyi said.
  • User-facing applications are increasingly embedding DeFi infrastructure to offer seamless financial services, a trend known as the "DeFi mullet," the report noted.
  • DeFi protocols are expanding into tokenized real-world assets, with crypto-native asset managers playing a key role in capital allocation and governance, it added.

data.title: SUI Reverses After Wild Swings; Trading Volume Spikes 11% Above 30-Day Average
data.description:
• SUI traded in a volatile 7.3% intraday range between $2.71 and $2.92 before closing near $2.78, according to CoinDesk Research's technical analysis model.
  • 24-hour trading volume surged 11% above the 30-day average, reflecting heavy participation during the reversal.
  • Price action failed to sustain above $2.82 resistance and found renewed support near $2.72 after multiple intraday pivots.
